content.title: Fix attribution pipeline false positive
content.summary: Investigation into a silent attribution pipeline failure. The attribution-tracer agent was spawned by the debug skill and claimed to have written a learning record to learnings/unified-learnings.jsonl, but no such record was written. Root cause is a three-layer failure: the debug skill gives no mechanism to derive transcript_path, the attribution-tracer has no input validation to catch a missing or unreadable transcript_path, and the attribution-tracer's summary output does not propagate the grounding judge's verdict — allowing it to claim success regardless of outcome. All judgment calls resolved. The fix spans attribution-tracer (input validation + honest summary) and the debug skill (transcript path resolution). The missing record itself was a valid attribution, so the learning was lost.
decisions: The attribution record for the missing invalidateQueries call was valid. T-0004 in SpawnedSapien's tasks file (20260604-1727-refund-system.json:81) has the criterion 'On success: Order Item shows refunded state without full page reload', which is underspecified and does not require cache invalidation. The record should have been written.

The false success claim originated in the attribution-tracer's summary output. The summary can say 'written' regardless of what the grounding judge returned, because the tracer does not propagate the judge's verdict.

Three-layer fix is required: (1) debug skill must derive and pass transcript_path explicitly when spawning the attribution-tracer, (2) attribution-tracer must fail-fast with an explicit error when transcript_path is absent or the file is unreadable, (3) attribution-tracer must end its summary with either 'WROTE: <id>' on confirmed write or 'NOT WRITTEN: <reason>' on rejection or error.
Fix priority: Layer 3 (summary honesty) first, Layer 2 (input validation) second, Layer 1 (transcript path derivation in debug skill) last.
log-learning.py writes to ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/learnings/unified-learnings.jsonl always. This is hardcoded and by design. Writes are never project-relative.

If the attribution had been written correctly, the grounding judge would have demoted confidence from confirmed to candidate because the tracer's quote ('shows refunded state without page reload') was a paraphrase of the actual criterion ('On success: Order Item shows refunded state without full page reload').
content.next_action: Run /to-tasks to generate task breakdown for the three-layer attribution pipeline fix
content.problem_statement: The attribution-tracer agent claims to write learning records but silently fails. When the grounding judge rejects a record (or log-learning.py errors), the tracer's summary still reports success. The debug skill also provides no concrete mechanism to derive transcript_path, so spawns can silently pass an absent or wrong path.
content.solution: Patch the attribution-tracer to validate transcript_path on entry and to end its summary with an explicit WROTE/NOT-WRITTEN verdict. Patch the debug skill's attribution-capture block to derive and pass transcript_path via the same env-var mechanism used by filter-session-transcript.sh.
content.evidence: Transcript review from a SpawnedSapien debug session showed the attribution-tracer claiming success, but ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/learnings/unified-learnings.jsonl contained no corresponding entry. The SpawnedSapien project has no learnings/ directory at all.
implementation_decisions: attribution-tracer: Add input validation before Step 1. If transcript_path is absent or the file at that path does not exist, return {"error": "transcript_path missing or unreadable: <path>"} and stop. Do not proceed to draft a record.

attribution-tracer: After the grounding judge returns, the final output summary must contain one of two explicit terminal lines: 'WROTE: <id>' (judge verdict pass and log-learning.py exited 0) or 'NOT WRITTEN: <reason>' (judge verdict rejected, log-learning.py errored, or input validation failed). Echo the judge's raw verdict JSON before the terminal line.

debug skill (attribution-capture block): Before spawning the attribution-tracer, resolve transcript_path using $CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR env vars (same mechanism as filter-session-transcript.sh). Pass the resolved absolute path explicitly in the agent prompt under the transcript_path key.
testing_decisions: No unit tests for infra (skill/agent) changes per project convention

out_of_scope.rationale: Writing to the shared learnings dir is by design. Centralizing learning records cross-project is the intended behavior.

out_of_scope.text: Change the grounding judge's paraphrase demotion threshold
out_of_scope.rationale: The existing behavior (demote to candidate on paraphrase) is correct and was working as designed in this case.

risks_and_tradeoffs: Adding fail-fast input validation to attribution-tracer means any session where transcript_path is not resolvable will produce no learning record at all rather than a candidate-confidence record. This is the correct tradeoff — a fabricated record is worse than no record.

tasks.title: Add explicit WROTE/NOT-WRITTEN verdict to attribution-tracer summary
tasks.description: Patch claude-code-shared/agents/attribution-tracer.md so the agent's final output is honest about whether the learning record was written. After the artifact-grounding-judge returns its verdict, the attribution-tracer must echo the judge's raw verdict JSON, then terminate with exactly one of: 'WROTE: <id>' (judge verdict pass AND log-learning.py exited 0) or 'NOT WRITTEN: <reason>' (judge verdict rejected, log-learning.py errored, or any earlier failure). The current behavior allows the summary to say 'written' regardless of outcome. This is the Layer 3 fix (summary honesty).
acceptance_criteria: attribution-tracer.md contains an explicit output contract section requiring the agent to end its summary with 'WROTE: <id>' or 'NOT WRITTEN: <reason>'
The output contract specifies that the judge's raw verdict JSON must be echoed before the terminal line
The output contract covers all three failure cases: verdict rejected, log-learning.py non-zero exit, and input validation failure (pre-emptive for T-0038)
A reviewer can confirm the change by reading attribution-tracer.md without running the agent

tasks.title: Add transcript_path input validation to attribution-tracer
tasks.description: Patch claude-code-shared/agents/attribution-tracer.md to add a Step 0 (before the existing Step 1 transcript read) that validates transcript_path. If transcript_path is absent from the input prompt or if the file at that path does not exist on disk, the agent must immediately return {"error": "transcript_path missing or unreadable: <path>"} and stop. It must not proceed to draft a record, draft evidence, or spawn the grounding judge. This is the Layer 2 fix (fail-fast on bad input). The terminal output rule from T-0037 applies: this exit path must emit 'NOT WRITTEN: transcript_path missing or unreadable'.
acceptance_criteria: attribution-tracer.md contains a Step 0 input validation block before Step 1
Step 0 specifies the exact error JSON to return when transcript_path is absent or unreadable
Step 0 specifies that the agent stops immediately — no record draft, no grounding judge spawn
The error path emits the NOT WRITTEN terminal line per the T-0037 output contract
A reviewer can confirm the change by reading attribution-tracer.md without running the agent

tasks.title: Add transcript_path resolution to debug skill attribution-capture block
tasks.description: Patch claude-code-shared/skills/debug/SKILL.md's attribution-capture block to derive and pass transcript_path explicitly before spawning the attribution-tracer agent. The current block says transcript_path is 'always available' but provides no mechanism to obtain it. Add a concrete bash command using $CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR env vars (the same mechanism filter-session-transcript.sh uses) to resolve the absolute path to the current session JSONL file. Require that the resolved path be passed explicitly in the agent spawn prompt under the transcript_path key. This is the Layer 1 fix (transcript path derivation at the call site).
acceptance_criteria: debug/SKILL.md attribution-capture block contains a concrete bash command to resolve transcript_path using $CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR before spawning the attribution-tracer
The block explicitly states that the resolved path must be passed in the agent prompt under the transcript_path key
The vague 'always available' language is replaced with the concrete resolution mechanism
A reviewer can confirm the change by reading debug/SKILL.md without running the skill
tasks.pr:
gh pr create --title "Fix attribution pipeline false positives" --body "$(cat <<EOF
### Fix attribution pipeline false positives

The attribution-tracer silently claimed success when the grounding judge rejected a record or log-learning.py errored. The debug skill also had no mechanism to resolve transcript_path, causing silent empty-path failures.

**Changes:**
- attribution-tracer: Add Step 0 input validation (fail-fast on missing/unreadable transcript_path)
- attribution-tracer: Add terminal output rule requiring WROTE/<id> or NOT WRITTEN/<reason> after judge verdict
- debug skill: Replace vague "always available" transcript_path note with concrete bash derivation using CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR
EOF
)"

follow_ups.title: Re-run attribution trace for the lost SpawnedSapien invalidateQueries learning
steps: Switch to the SpawnedSapien project directory: cd /Users/eric/Documents/dev/personal/SpawnedSapien

Open a new Claude Code session in SpawnedSapien and invoke /debug with the confirmed issue: 'useRefundOrderItem.ts never called queryClient.invalidateQueries after success, causing item.refundedAt to stay null until page reload. Fix: void queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["orderHistory"] }) after setStatus("done") in useRefundOrderItem.ts. Root criterion: T-0004 acceptance criterion "On success: Order Item shows refunded state without full page reload" was underspecified.'
Verify the attribution-tracer summary ends with 'WROTE: <id>' (not a fabricated success claim)
